title: Police community support officers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of security workers and security guards. All protective service supervisors not listed separately above. Patrol assigned area to prevent fish and game law violations. Investigate reports of damage to crops or property by wildlife. Compile biological data. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Determine response requirements and relative priorities of situations, and dispatch units according to established procedures.
Recommend new or improved processes or equipment to reduce risk exposure and potential financial fraud losses.
Learn the location and purpose of street traffic signs, and discuss traffic routing plans and control-point locations with superiors.
Stop speeding vehicles to warn drivers of traffic laws and inform them of detour routes through construction sites.
Direct traffic movement, warn of hazards, and guide pedestrians or vehicles across streets as necessary.
Investigate known or suspected internal theft, external theft, or vendor fraud, and conduct store audits to identify problem areas.
Collaborate with law enforcement agencies to report or investigate crimes, and assist with staff selection, training, and supervision.
Maintain documentation or reports on security-related incidents, and monitor compliance with operating procedures for loss prevention.
Inspect buildings, equipment, or access points to determine security risks, and verify proper functioning of physical security systems.
Respond to critical incidents, such as catastrophic events, and testify in civil or criminal court proceedings.
Patrol assigned areas by car, boat, or on foot to enforce game, fish, or boating laws and manage wildlife programs.
Investigate and report crop, property, or habitat damage and instances of water pollution to determine causes and preventive measures.
Issue warnings, citations, or licenses and permits as necessary to ensure compliance with laws or regulations.
Collect and report information on populations or conditions of fish and wildlife, and supervise seasonal workers' activities.
Develop and document security procedures, policies, or standards, and maintain close communications with dispatching personnel.
Write reports documenting observations made while on patrol.
Assign security personnel to posts or patrols and recruit, interview, and hire security personnel.
Explain company policies and procedures to staff using oral or written communication.
Inspect and adjust security equipment to ensure it is operational or detect evidence of tampering.
Investigate disturbances on the premises, including security alarms, altercations, and suspicious activity.
Log items distributed to persons, such as keys and key cards, and monitor and authorize entry of employees and visitors.
Monitor closed-circuit television cameras and the behavior of security employees to ensure adherence to standards.
Patrol the premises to prevent or detect intrusion, protect property, or preserve order.
Schedule training or drills for emergencies, such as fires, bombs, and other threats, and train security personnel on related procedures.
Screen individuals and belongings to prevent passage of prohibited materials using detection devices.
Secure entrances and exits by locking doors and gates.
Order materials or supplies, such as keys, uniforms, and badges, and present budgets to upper management.

emerging_tasks.task: Deliver educational presentations on wildlife topics, such as animal safety, at schools and community events.
Operate drones for surveillance of large areas and tracking of wildlife.
Perform traffic control duties such as setting up barricades and temporary signs, placing bags on parking meters to limit their use, or directing traffic or pedestrians.
Pick up deceased animals from roadways and dispose of the animal carcasses.
Testify at court hearings when citations are contested.
Transport injured or ill wildlife to rescues.
Use drone technology for surveillance and loss prevention.
